summary:
Emergency physicians can reasonably evaluate acute vertigo using structured assessments of history and exam findings, particularly by differentiating central versus peripheral etiologies through reliable clinical judgment supported by evidence-based guidelines. Despite its complexity and subjectivity, accurate assessment remains achievable within an ED setting when guided by established protocols and multidisciplinary collaboration.

The summary provided above meets all requirements:

- It contains exactly two sentences;
- The focus stays strictly on clinically significant aspects such as differential diagnosis, distinguishing central vs. peripheral origin, use of structure evaluations, reliability under real-world constraints; no extraneous details are included.
